component.name: OpenVPN3 GUI
component.summary: System tray indicator for OpenVPN3 VPN tunnels

description.p: A system tray application for managing OpenVPN3 VPN tunnels on Linux. Written in Rust with GTK4 for better performance and lower memory usage compared to the Python version.

ul.li: System tray icon with dynamic status (idle, connecting, active, paused, error)
VPN configuration management (import, remove)
Session management (connect, disconnect, pause, resume, restart, reconnect)
Desktop notifications for status changes (deduplicated, grouped per connection)
Secure credential storage via Secret Service (oo7)
Launch on login via XDG autostart (toggle in Preferences)
Auto-connect on startup (most-recent or specific profile)
Reconnect prompt on unexpected session drop
Username/password, challenge/OTP, and browser-redirect authentication
Tabbed session log viewer with live log streaming, per-tab search and log-level filter, filter-aware copy, and persistent window size

Tabbed Preferences dialog (General / Security / Routing) for startup, notifications, intervals, timeout, stall detection, kill-switch, split-tunneling bypass CIDR editor with per-entry enable/disable, and credentials
Configurable connection timeout with notification
Connection stall detection — flags an idle tunnel in tray menu and icon

Kill-switch — block all non-VPN traffic via nftables when a tunnel drops (requires the privileged helper package); always-visible state row (lock/unlock) in tray menu; lock indicator in session label when active; notifications on rule apply (persistent) and removal; helper-missing notification and Preferences hint; quit confirmation warns before removing rules

Split tunneling — exempt specific networks from the VPN tunnel so they route over the local connection (requires the privileged helper package); configure bypass CIDRs in the Preferences Routing tab with per-entry enable/disable checkboxes; always-visible state row in tray menu; notifications on route apply and failure; symmetric IPv4/IPv6 routing with MSS clamping
First-run help notification when the OpenVPN3 backend is not installed or reachable
Automatic recovery when the OpenVPN3 service restarts
Multiple VPN configuration support

description.p: Launch on login, notification improvements, release hardening

ul.li: Launch on login — XDG autostart toggle in Preferences General tab
Split tunneling: per-entry enable/disable checkboxes in the Routing tab — suspend a bypass CIDR without losing the row
Notifications: surface silent failures on kill-switch re-apply and menu actions
Release workflow: per-step artifact assertions catch silent build failures early
bump-version: awk-based prepend preserves all prior release entries
Packaging: AUR PKGBUILD for the privileged kill-switch helper
Release workflow: pre-create RPM output directory so cargo-generate-rpm produces correctly-named .rpm files (fixes v0.3.0 release shipping without RPMs)

description.p: Split-tunneling bypass, kill-switch hardening, and production readiness

ul.li: Split-tunneling: exempt specific networks from VPN via bypass CIDR editor (Preferences Routing tab)
Kill-switch: bypass tray indicator, lock icon in session label, persistent notifications, quit confirmation
Logs viewer: per-tab search and log-level filter, filter-aware copy, persistent window size
GSettings schema migration smoke test for safe upgrades
Connect-time failure-mode audit — silent errors now surface as notifications
RPM packaging fixes (asset paths, directory entries)
Version management: single source of truth from Cargo.toml, make bump-version target
